name: rename-a-task
goal: Rename the Visual design task to a new label.

steps.note: Select the task I want to rename.
TASK context: is there a clearly labelled Rename verb, distinct from Edit (SR-IXC-16, SR-IXC-17)?
Naive first attempt: double-click the bar to edit its name in place (SR-IXC-20).

Did an inline in-place label editor open, or did the properties card open instead? Double-clicking the body opening a card (not an inline rename) is a finding against the Rename/Edit verb split (SR-IXC-16, SR-IXC-17).
Press Esc to cancel whatever opened and restore the prior state (SR-IXC-07 uniform cancel).
Was the editor dismissed with no change committed?
Make sure the task is still selected before using the app bar verb.
Use the Rename button: per the consistent-verb rule it must start an inline in-place edit on the label, never a card (SR-IXC-16).
Is an inline editor now open directly on the task label?
Type a new name for the task.
Does the inline editor show the typed text live?
Enter commits the change, matching commit semantics used by every editor surface (SR-IXC-07).
Was the label renamed and committed on the bar?
Esc to deselect and return to the default context (SR-IXC-09).
Confirm a neutral default state with the rename persisted.
